title: Death rate due to low physical activity
subtitle: Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to [low physical activity](#dod:low_physical_activity) per 100,000 people.
note: To allow for comparisons between countries and over time, this metric is [age-standardized](#dod:age_standardized).
caption:
Death rate due to low physical activity

Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to low physical activity per 100,000 people.

This choropleth world map displays 2023 death rates attributed to low physical activity, measured as the estimated annual number of deaths per 100,000 people. The legend ranges from 0 to over 30, with specific tick marks at 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, and 30. The darkest green band, representing values above 25, is concentrated in the Middle East, where Saudi Arabia, Iraq, and Kuwait clearly fall into this highest visible category. Surrounding nations in the region, including Iran and parts of North Africa, appear in the next darkest band, sitting between 20 and 25. In contrast, the United States, Canada, Australia, and most of Western Europe sit in the lightest shades of the legend, indicating rates near the bottom of the scale, generally below 5 or 10. South America and parts of Eastern Europe display a mix of light to mid-range green shades, clustering mostly between 5 and 15. Sub-Saharan Africa shows variation, with South Africa appearing in a darker mid-range band while many neighboring countries remain in lighter shades. Greenland is marked with a hatched pattern indicating no data. The metric is age-standardized to allow for comparisons between countries and over time. The visual distribution highlights significantly higher rates in the Middle East compared to the lighter shades observed across the Americas, Oceania, and much of Europe.
